title: Rulf Yage
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Moffyage.jpg]] |name=Rulf Yage |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=Blond |feathers= |eyes=[[Blue]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners=[[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] {{C|wife}}[[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]] |siblings= |children=[[Gunn Yage]] |affiliation=*[[Fel Empire]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] *[[Empire-in-exile]][[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|As a starfighter pilot, you anticipated your enemy's moves. You never applied that to politics. Nor would you let me help you. That's why Morlish Veed is the High Moff and you aren't.|Nyna Calixte to Rulf Yage|}} '''Rulf Yage''' was a hardliner [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] and a decorated [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] during the time of the [[Fel Empire]] in [[130 ABY]]. As a member of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], Yage originally served as the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]], an elite unit of [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class starfighters]] assigned to defend the Imperial capital [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], before being assigned as the coordinator of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] tactics on the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Ephin Sarreti (Star Destroyer)|''Ephin Sarreti'']], attached to the [[Second Outer Rim Fleet]]. As his career continued, Yage became a member of the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]], representing the interests of the Navy. He married fellow Moff [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] at one point in his life. Calixte eventually left Yage for [[High Moff]] [[Morlish Veed]], but not before the marriage produced a daughter, [[Gunn Yage|Gunn]], whom Rulf treated as the son he always wanted. During the waning years of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], the Moff Council allied themselves with [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] Order, a move carried out behind the back of the incumbent [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]. With the [[Sith/Legends|Sith's]] backing, the Moff Council invoked the [[Treaty of Anaxes]] and declared [[Sith–Imperial War|war]] on the Alliance, which culminated in a [[Battle of Caamas|decisive Imperial victory]] above the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]] that saw the Alliance surrender to Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Morlish Veed. Yage was tasked by the Sith with leading an [[Massacre at Ossus|Imperial attack]] on the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. For his part in the victory, Yage was named the "[[Rulf Yage|Hero of Ossus]]" and made a Moff. He returned to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the new capital of the Empire, where he witnessed [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt [[Attack on Emperor Fel|usurp]] control of the Empire, killing a [[Roan Fel's double|decoy]] of Roan Fel. Krayt forced the Moff Council into subservience, and Yage and the others complied. During the hunt for Jedi-turned-[[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]] in [[137 ABY]], Yage and the other Moffs plotted to use Skywalker to assassinate Emperor Krayt. However, Skywalker had already been captured by the Sith, and was being held within [[Temple of the Sith|their temple]] on Coruscant. He ultimately [[Skirmish at the Temple of the Sith|escaped]] captivity and fled Coruscant aboard his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], after Yage was forced to recall the pursuing Skull Squadron so as not to violate the Sith airspace around the temple. At a later meeting of the Moff Council, Yage was assigned by [[Regent/Legends|Regent]] Veed to lead Sith-Imperial forces in [[Operation: Thunderstroke]], an attempt to break up a [[Mission to Agamar|meeting]] between Fel's Empire and the [[New Jedi Order]] and capture the Emperor-in-exile. Yage assumed command of the [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]], leading it from the Star Destroyer ''[[War Hammer]]''. Emperor Fel escaped, but his daughter, [[Marasiah Fel|Marasiah]], was captured. Yage brought her to Coruscant as a prisoner before being assigned to lead the Strikeforce against those suspected of harboring [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] refugees on the [[Da Soocha]]n [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Napdu]]. Yage later participated in the Sith-Imperial [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|assault]] on the Jedi Order's [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]], and it was during this battle that he and his daughter led many Sith-Imperials in defecting to the Empire-in-exile. As an admiral, Yage led ships of Fel's Empire in the allied [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|attack]] on Coruscant, which proved to be the final battle of the [[Second Imperial Civil War|Imperial civil war]]. ==Biography== ===Naval career=== {{Quote|Spast, Gunner, how did you survive your childhood?''"
"''Cut him ''some'' slack, Storm. He was expecting a boy and instead he got ''me''! And then my ''mother''…|Jae "Storm" Akura and Gunn "Gunner" Yage, on Moff Yage|}} Rulf Yage, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was born into the famed [[Yage family]].''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' He attended the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] before starting off his career in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] of the [[Fel Empire]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. Yage became a member of [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]], an elite [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class starfighter]] squadron which was assigned to the defense of the Imperial capital [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], and he soon rose to the position of [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]]. He married [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]], the eventual [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]], and the relationship resulted in a daughter, [[Gunn Yage|Gunn]], whom Rulf raised as the son he always wanted.[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] After serving in Skull Squadron, Yage was assigned to the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Ephin Sarreti (Star Destroyer)|''Ephin Sarreti'']], part of the Imperial [[Second Outer Rim Fleet]]. While aboard, he was responsible for coordinating the fleet's starfighter tactics. Yage was eventually promoted to the rank of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], but around the same time, Calixte left him for [[High Moff]] [[Morlish Veed]], also a member of the Navy. In an effort to overcome the growing amount of bitterness in his life, Yage embedded himself in his duties. As a hardliner Imperial, Yage was among those who objected to [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s announcement that his daughter, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]], would succeed him as leader of the Empire. ===Seat on the Council of Moffs=== {{Quote|So, you're a hotshot ''Jedi''. Thought those losers were all dead. Guess my father ''missed'' a few on Ossus.''"
"''Your ''father''? Yage? Sithspit! Your father is the "''Butcher'' of Ossus"?!|Gunn Yage and Cade Skywalker|[[Legacy (2006) 39|''Legacy'' (2006) 39]]}} [[File:FehlaaurYage.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Moffs Fehlaaur and Yage during a meeting of the Council of Moffs]] When the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance's]] [[Ossus Project]], an effort by the [[Shaper caste]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] to restore life to [[Planet/Legends|planets]] destroyed in their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]],''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' began to harm the inhabitants of the devastated worlds, the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] decided to invoke the [[Treaty of Anaxes]] and declare war on the Alliance in [[127 ABY]]. [[128 ABY|A year later]], the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]], without the knowledge of Emperor Fel, allied with [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith|Sith Order]]—the very group of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] who had sabotaged the Ossus Project. The [[Sith–Imperial War]] reached its final stage by [[130 ABY]], when above the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] Veed's forces brought about a [[Battle of Caamas|decisive Imperial victory]] when he forced the Alliance commanders into surrendering. [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|fell to the Empire]] around the same time. The members of the Alliance governing [[Triumvirate (Galactic Alliance)|Triumvirate]] became nothing more than puppets to ensure the peaceful transition from Galactic Alliance to Galactic Empire; Emperor Fel then made them [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroys]] over what was left of [[Galactic Alliance Space|Alliance Space]].[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] The final act of the war, however, had yet to come. Yage, now a Grand Admiral, led an [[Massacre at Ossus|attack]] on the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] alongside [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Nihl]]. [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] and Sith alike eradicated the majority of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] present, including [[Jedi High Council/Legends|High Council]] member and champion of the Ossus Project, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kol Skywalker]].[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] For his part in the massacre, Yage—who was praised as the "[[Rulf Yage|Hero of Ossus]]"[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]]—was appointed Moff and given a seat on the Moff Council, where he represented the interests of the Imperial Navy. The Council met on conquered Coruscant at a point following the massacre, and while Moff Veed was concluding his report to the Council and Emperor Fel on the events on Ossus, [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt and three of his minions—[[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Wyyrlok]], [[Darth Maladi|Maladi]], and Nihl—entered Fel's throne room and proceeded to [[Attack on Emperor Fel|seize control]] of the Empire. The Dark Lord [[Death/Legends|slayed]] the four [[Imperial Knight]] bodyguards present before assassinating the man who sat on the throne. Though Krayt had only killed a [[Roan Fel's double|decoy]], the Moffs were quick to pledge their loyalty to their new Emperor, Krayt. ===The search for Skywalker=== [[File:LovingFather-SWL14p15.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Rulf disciplines his daughter Gunn.]] {{Quote|For example, this report states that Skull Squadron—once your command, and now your ''daughter's''—spotted and ''lost'' Skywalker in the Undercity. Should we assume something from ''that''?''"
"''Read the ''rest'' of the report. The identification of that ship was never ''satisfactorily'' confirmed.|Moffs Fehlaaur and Yage|}} [[137 ABY|Seven years]] into his reign as Emperor, Krayt began to realize that his body was failing him.[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]] He had become aware of the [[Force healing/Legends|healing power]] that Jedi-turned-[[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]] possessed through Moff [[Konrad Rus]]'s contacts on Bastion, where Skywalker had returned Imperial heir Princess Marasiah Fel after she had been injured during an [[Skirmish on Vendaxa|encounter]] with the Sith on [[Vendaxa/Legends|Vendaxa]]. The Dark Lord of the Sith hoped to capture the former Jedi and convert him to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] with the hope that his abilities could be used to increase the Emperor's own longevity.[[Legacy (2006) 7|''Legacy'' (2006) 7]] Skywalker soon arrived on Coruscant in his [[Helot-class medium space transport|''Helot''-class transport]], the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], and there he was pursued by Skull Squadron under the command of Moff Yage's daughter, Gunn "Gunner" Yage. On orders from the Moff to take Skywalker alive, Gunn and her pilots ultimately lost the ''Mynock'' when Skywalker hid it in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant Undercity]]. Rulf awaited his daughter in the [[Skull Squadron Hangar]] when Gunn and her squad mates [[Jae Akura]], [[Brodie Coburn]], and [[Tev Rimon]] returned. Wasting no time in scolding his daughter, he announced that the loss of the ''Mynock'' had been unacceptable. Gunn replied that the ship's identification had not been confirmed, and that their failure was inevitable, as they would not have been able to take out the ship without risking Skywalker's death and therefore disobeying Rulf's own edict. The Moff began berating his daughter—he had issued an order, and had no desire to listen to their excuses for failing. Gunn proposed that, should her father be dissatisfied with her handling of the squadron, he could dismiss her whenever he wished. Rather than do so, he ordered his daughter to attend dinner with him the following evening—and not to be late. Akura was astonished that Gunn had been able to survive her childhood, but she stood up for her father, noting that he ''had'' wanted a son. Skywalker ultimately fell into Krayt's hands when he was captured by [[Hand (One Sith)|Hands]] [[Darth Talon|Talon]] and Nihl after infiltrating the [[Temple of the Sith]] and attempting to free the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] Jedi [[Hosk Trey'lis]]. At a meeting of the Moff Council the following day, Darth Maladi informed the Moffs that the search for Skywalker had been called off, but she refused to divulge any information despite Moff Calixte's assumption that Skywalker had been captured. As the discussions continued after Maladi cut her transmission, Yage, speaking to the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] Moff [[Fehlaaur'aitel'loro|Fehlaaur]], accused the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] of harboring the deposed Emperor Fel and his forces. Fehlaaur and Yage bickered over the reported sighting of the ''Mynock'' in the Coruscant Undercity until Moff [[Geist (Moff)|Geist]] of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] intervened, announcing that Skywalker's whereabouts were no longer relevant now that the search for the former Jedi had ended. Geist realized that the Council commanded less power than they had had under Fel, and Yage responded that their current predicament had been the result of Calixte's machinations with the Sith to bring them in on the Empire's side during the Sith-Imperial War. Calixte tried to defend her actions by reporting that the war—which the Council had wanted in the first place—had been going poorly for the Empire. Fehlaaur and Geist offered further opinions on the matter, but Morlish Veed put a halt to the debate and called for unity before revealing his intentions to capture Skywalker for the Council's own purposes against Krayt's Order. When Geist and Fehlaaur departed, Yage left for dinner with his daughter, leaving Veed and Calixte to discuss their own plans. [[File:RulfsIsNotToQuestionWhySWL19p13.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Yage gives the order to recall the Skulls.]] Yage met his daughter at the [[Naboo Queen (restaurant)|Naboo Queen]] restaurant, formerly the senatorial apartment of one-time [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] [[Queen/Legends|queen]] and [[Senator/Legends|senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] during the final days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].{{Film|II}} There, the two discussed the loyalty of Gunn's Skull Squadron before they were interrupted by Moff Calixte, Rulf's one-time wife and Gunn's mother. Calixte had come to question her daughter on Skull Squadron's chase of the ''Mynock'', but the meeting quickly dissolved into arguing and name-calling. Rulf calmly ate his soup as Calixte and Gunn's debate continued, interjecting only when necessary—at one point, he reminded his daughter that Calixte was still a superior officer and deserved some respect. As Calixte departed, she commented on how nice their civil discussions could be; Rulf replied that they were more like civil wars, to the amusement of his daughter. As Skywalker's [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] continued, the former Jedi managed to convince the Sith that he was willing to join them. But eventually, in Darth Krayt's throne room in the [[Temple of the Sith]] on Coruscant, he [[Skirmish at the Temple of the Sith|dueled]] Darth Nihl, the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] Sith Lord who had killed his father, and defeated him. Krayt urged Skywalker to kill Nihl, but Skywalker refused and instead attacked Krayt. In the atmosphere around the Temple, the ''Mynock''—crewed by Skywalker's cohorts [[Deliah Blue]] and [[Jariah Syn]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chak (Wookiee)|Chak]], and the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Kee (Devaronian)|Kee]] of the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[YX-1980 space transport|ship]] ''[[Grinning Liar]]''—engaged Skull Squadron in an attempt to rescue Skywalker from the Temple. As Gunn locked on to the ''Mynock'', Moff Yage was ordered by an Imperial admiral via hologram to call off the squadron, in compliance with a standing order that no Imperial vessels were to violate the Temple's airspace. Yage reluctantly complied, ordering his aide Captain [[Skolitz]] to recall the Skulls. Skywalker and his allies ultimately fled Coruscant, on the order of Cade's mother Morrigan Corde—an [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] alias of Moff Calixte.[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]] ===Operation: Thunderstroke=== {{Quote|Look at them, Fehlaaur. Look at my "''command''." I no longer serve the Empire… I've become a leader of monsters.|Moff Yage|[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]]}} Some time following Skywalker's escape from Coruscant, newly-appointed [[Regent/Legends|Regent]] Veed received intelligence of a planned [[Mission to Agamar|meeting]] between members of the [[New Jedi Order]] and representatives of the Empire-in-exile—led by the exiled Emperor Fel himself—on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]]. Veed revealed this information to his comrades at a meeting of the Moff Council. Determined to break up the rendezvous and end the [[Second Imperial Civil War|Imperial civil war]], he launched [[Operation: Thunderstroke]], with the primary objective of capturing Fel so that he could stand trial and be subsequently executed. The regent assigned Yage as commander of the Sith-Imperial forces participating in the operation.[[Legacy (2006) 43|''Legacy'' (2006) 43]] [[File:Yage_fehlaaur.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Moff Yage addresses Sith pilots of the Strikeforce.]] With fellow Moff Fehlaaur serving as his [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|lieutenant commander]], Yage assumed command of the Star Destroyer ''[[War Hammer]]'', using it as the flagship of his [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]]. While the ''War Hammer'' was in deep in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], en route to its destination, Yage briefed a squad of Sith pilots in preparation for the upcoming assault. Yage informed them that Fel had been located and was to be captured alive. The Moff tasked a number of Sith pilots with flying their [[Fury-class starfighter|''Fury''-class starfighters]] into battle as the first wave, leading Predator fighters in crippling all of Fel's ships and trapping the Emperor-in-exile on Agamar. Yage instructed them that, with the exceptions of Fel and his daughter, no one was to be left alive. After telling them that their success was compulsory, he dismissed them. Once they were gone, Yage spoke to Fehlaaur, telling the Chiss that he believed he had gone from serving the Empire to leading monsters. Yage was later present in the ''War Hammer''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] speaking to Sith pilot [[Darth Rauder]], who had been placed in command of Skull Squadron for the Agamar campaign. Gunn arrived to speak with her father about Morrigan Corde[[Legacy (2006) 45|''Legacy'' (2006) 45]]—whom Gunn had heard on a previous assignment was really her mother, Moff Calixte.[[Legacy (2006) 40|''Legacy'' (2006) 40]] When Gunn entered the hangar, she noticed that Skull Squadron's Predator fighters had been replaced by a [[Sith-Imperial starfighter|new design]]. Moff Yage promptly introduced Rauder and explained that the Predators had been replaced on Rauder's command. Gunn and the Sith engaged in a brief argument over the decision, but Gunn backed down when Rauder threatened to execute anyone who questioned her orders. The Sith departed, leaving Moff Yage to question his daughter as to why she was making his life more difficult. Gunn proceeded to ask for her father's consul on her dilemma, careful to not reveal names or too many details. She told him that while she wanted to act on her uncertain information and damage the person involved, doing so could easily damage others. The Moff correctly deduced that his daughter was referring to Corde and suggested that she use her training as a fighter pilot to aid her in solving the problem. He noted that killing shots were efficient if planned properly, but could never be taken back—something he had learned the hard way on Ossus. Yage quickly changed the subject, announcing that they would be reaching Agamar shortly and that Gunn should familiarize her squad with the controls of their new fighters. While the delegates representing the Empire-in-exile and the New Jedi Order met on Agamar, the First Sith Imperial Strikeforce emerged from hyperspace into the [[Mirgoshir system/Legends|system]] and deployed its fighter squadrons. The Fel Imperial Star Destroyer [[Dauntless (Pellaeon-class)|''Dauntless'']], commanded by Admiral [[Tohri Challon]], did likewise, moving into broadside combat with the Sith Imperial destroyers while the squadrons engaged one another. The ''War Hammer'' destroyed the ''Dauntless'', but it was unable to stop Fel from escaping to hyperspace in his [[Defender One|shuttle]].[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] Princess Marasiah Fel, however, was captured by the Sith Imperial forces. Acting on orders from Regent Veed, Moff Yage brought the Princess to Coruscant, arriving at the capital one week after the battle had concluded.[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]] ===Under the Sith's command=== {{Quote|We've lost any control we ever had, Veed! The Sith twist those who ''should'' serve the Empire to serve ''their'' will! Soon we will all call ourselves ''"Sith"!''|Moff Yage|}} When their [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] landed, Yage escorted the Princess off the shuttle. She attempted to convince the Moff to release her and even defect, citing his prior service to Emperor Fel and his dissatisfaction for the Sith and their actions. Yage refused, knowing that his defection would only bring hardships down on his daughter. The Princess answered that she had misjudged him, saying that Yage lacked both courage and honor. The two were approached by the [[Sith Inquisitor|Inquisitor]] [[Darth Havok]], who demanded that the Moff hand Fel over to him for transport to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] per Darth Wyyrlok's orders. Yage refused, citing the source of his own instructions, but even the arrival of Regent Veed and Moff Calixte did not convince Havok to back down. The Inquisitor escorted the Princess away, leaving the three Moffs to bicker among themselves. [[File:Yage_da_soocha.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Yage reflects on the victory at Da Soocha.]] Veed wondered how Yage could have botched the mission, but Yage defended himself, declaring that they had been betrayed and that they had at least succeeded in capturing Princess Fel. Yage vented his dislike for the Sith and their policies, specifically how they manipulated all of the Empire's servants into becoming minions of the Sith. Veed warned him of [[Treason/Legends|treason]], but Yage quickly shot back, telling the Regent that they would just as quickly dispose of him if they saw the need. Turning to Calixte, Yage said that he would tell Gunn that Calixte had asked after her, noting that Gunn had only ever received such "civil lies" from them. Their conversation over, Yage left to ready the Strikeforce for immediate departure for the [[Da Soocha]]n [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Napdu]]. For this mission, however, his superiors would be the Sith Lord [[Darth Azard]] and [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Vul Isen]], the being responsible for the [[Final Protocol|destruction]] of all life on [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. A few days later, the ''War Hammer'' arrived in the [[Cyax system/Legends|Cyax system]], settling into [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] above Napdu. Azard and Isen contacted the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Azzim Anjiliac Atirue]], who was believed to be hiding [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] refugees from Dac. When the Hutt proved uncooperative, Azard had Yage deploy the [[Sith–Imperial attack ship|bomber]] group [[Quad Victor]] to release the toxin responsible for Dac's destruction onto Da Soocha, followed by the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the moon Napdu and the deployment of fighters to obliterate any craft attempting to flee the Imperial assault. The Moff was stunned by Azard's orders, believing it all to be overkill, but Yage complied nevertheless. When the bombers completed their mission, Yage ordered the ''War Hammer''{{'s}} turbolasers to open fire on the moon while Skull Squadron intercepted and destroyed any and all craft escaping the carnage. When all was complete, Isen had Yage order a recall before congratulating the Moff on his success. The scientist proclaimed Yage as the "Conqueror of Da Soocha" while the Moff reflected on the victory. In [[138 ABY]], following Darth Krayt's return to power after his apparent death, Yage and the ''War Hammer'' participated in the Sith-Imperial [[Battle of Borosk (138 ABY)|recapture]] of the planet [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]]. After the battle, Yage was contacted by Grand Moff Veed, who was under the assumption that he had successfully executed Moff Calixte, Fel's spy within the Sith-Imperial ranks. In his transmission, Veed revealed the details of his encounter with Calixte, and the Grand Moff inquired if Yage had been aware of his former wife's true loyalties. Yage merely responded that Veed, having been Calixte's lover, had been unaware of her duplicity as well. Unsatisfied with Yage's response, Veed threatened the Moff with severe consequences if he could prove that Yage had had knowledge of Calixte's activities. When the transmission ended, Yage revealed to his daughter that he was greatly affected by the news, however inaccurate, of his ex-wife's death, despite the lack of feelings they had had for one another following their marriage.[[Legacy — War 2|''Legacy — War'' 2]] ===Defection=== {{Quote|This is Moff Ranulf ''[sic]'' Yage, ''formerly'' of the Sith Imperial Navy. My daughter is right. The Sith have no honor. I am joining her defection to His Imperial Majesty, Roan Fel of Bastion—if he will have me. Other captains of the Sith Empire who wish to join me should signify so immediately or be blown out of the sky as hostile vessels. Yage out.|Yage, in an open transmission to all ships participating in the assault on Taivas|}} Acting on intelligence gathered from the interrogation of [[Imperial Knight]] [[Antares Draco]], the Sith-Imperial forces launched a [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|strike]] on the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]], a secret Jedi refuge located on the planet [[Taivas]]. Krayt charged Moff Yage with command of the Imperial fleet, though overall command of the engagement fell to Grand Admiral Veed. In the initial phases of the battle, Yage's forces unleashed an orbital bombardment on Taivas, but magnetic interference from the planet disrupted the Imperials' targeting computers, resulting in casualties from friendly-fire and no damage to the Hidden Temple. Resorting to a ground assault led by Moff Geist, the Sith-Imperials engaged the Jedi defenders of the Hidden Temple. In the midst of the attack, however, the combined fleets of the Galactic Alliance and the Empire-in-exile emerged from hyperspace, trapping the Sith-Imperial fleet between them and the planet.[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] [[File:Yages_Defection.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Yage observes as his daughter defects to the Empire-in-exile.]] Krayt had anticipated such a maneuver, and responded with the deployment of his force of [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith troopers]]. The arriving Sith reinforcements cut through allied and Sith-Imperial forces alike, inflicting severe casualties on both sides of the battle. The troopers' disregard for their own allies prompted both Moff and Captain Yage to reconsider their own allegiances. Gunn took the opportunity to kill Darth Rauder during a dogfight; she announced her decision to defect to Fel's forces to her father aboard the ''War Hammer''. As his Sith overseer ordered the fleet to shoot down the captain, Moff Yage withdrew a blaster pistol and shot the Sith dead. Spurred by his daughter's decision, Yage sent out a holotransmission to both the Sith-Imperial and allied fleets, declaring that he and the crew of the ''War Hammer'' were pledging their loyalty to Fel, the legitimate emperor. He called upon other officers of the Sith Empire to follow suit or risk being destroyed; many ships answered the Moff's summons. Imperial Knight [[Treis Sinde]], having heard Yage's announcement, tasked a subordinate with contacting the Moff to officially welcome him to the Empire-in-exile. However, with the onslaught of the Sith troopers, any hope of defending the Hidden Temple was lost, and the Alliance, Jedi, and Empire-in-exile forces were compelled to flee with their new allies to Bastion. At Bastion, the factions of Fel's alliance massed for a [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|decisive attack]] on Coruscant, hoping that such a move would catch Krayt's Empire unaware and turn the tide of the war in the allies' favor. Yage, as admiral, served as one of the fleet commanders in charge of the assault on the capital, though overall command fell upon the senior Jedi. As preparations for the engagement continued, Yage and his daughter were joined by Morrigan Corde. Having abandoned her alter ego of Nyna Calixte, Corde revealed the details of her past to her former husband, including her ties to the Skywalker family, before stating that she was not looking for the Moff's forgiveness. Yage suggested that they discuss the revelation in detail some other time, but he did express his relief at seeing his ex-wife alive after Veed's attempt on her life. Shortly thereafter, Corde and the crew of the ''Mynock'' departed for Coruscant, leading a strike team to deactivate the capital's orbital defenses in preparation for the attack.[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]] While the fleet made its final preparations, Admiral Yage stressed coordination against the Sith troopers and their [[Annihilator-class starfighter|Annihilator starfighters]] while in conference with Admiral Stazi and Emperor Fel. The commanders agreed with Yage's decision to utilize the allies' combined fighter squadrons against the Annihilators and Coruscant's operational ground defenses. With the battle plan hammered out, the fleet jumped into the Coruscant system just as Corde's strike force succeeded in its objective. Assisted by Jedi-produced [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], the armada engaged the Sith-Imperial fleet, with Yage directing Skull and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadrons]] to take out gun emplacements on the planet's surface. As the orbital battle raged on, Corde sneaked into the Imperial headquarters and killed Veed; Skywalker, meanwhile, set off to assassinate Krayt. The allied fleet suffered heavy casualties, pushing its commanders to the limit. Yage tried to raise Emperor Fel on the [[Jagged Fel (starship)|Imperial flagship]] in the midst of the engagement, but the Emperor made no response, as he was preoccupied with thoughts of deploying the [[Bioweapon/Legends|bioweapon]] [[Omega Red]] onto the capital. Fel's obsession with killing Krayt and the Sith drove him to the dark side of the Force; Imperial Knight Antares Draco was forced to intervene, killing the Emperor and saving the lives of the allied forces who would have perished had Fel deployed the bioweapon. Skywalker, meanwhile, killed Krayt once and for all, effectively ending the war as the remaining Sith went into hiding. In the battle's aftermath, a [[Funeral of Roan Fel|funeral service]], which Yage attended, was held for Fel on liberated Coruscant. It was at this funeral that the galaxy was formally reorganized under the authority of the new [[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]].[[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy — War'' 6]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|When I issue an order, Captain, I do not look for ''excuses'' as to why my pilots ''failed'' to carry it out! Excuses are for ''weaklings''!|Rulf Yage, to his daughter Gunn|}} Moff Yage, a loyal Imperial, came from a notable family. Yage was not as cunning and devious as the other members of the Moff Council, something that his former wife Nyna Calixte would often point out to him. It was because of those traits, she claimed, that Morlish Veed was the High Moff rather than her former husband. Following his separation from Calixte, Yage led a bitter life, and he tried to hide that by throwing himself into his duties in the Navy. He was a skilled starfighter pilot and tactician, as shown by his command of the elite Skull Squadron and his coordination of fighter tactics for the Second Outer Rim Fleet. His skills eventually earned him the rank of Grand Admiral and, after his successful attack on the Jedi Temple on Ossus, a seat on the Council of Moffs. He was known to be one of the hardliner Moffs, objecting to Emperor Fel's decision to have his daughter, Princess Marasiah Fel, succeed him as ruler of the Empire. Additionally, the Moff expected all his directives to be carried out to the letter, and he had no time for excuses when those under his command were unable to follow those orders. In his opinion, only weak beings incapable of following instructions would attempt to justify their failure. When Gunn Yage and Cade Skywalker were stranded on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Gunn made an offhand remark to Skywalker about her father missing a few Jedi on Ossus. Skywalker was shocked to learn that Gunn was the daughter of the "Butcher of Ossus," but Gunn was quick to defend her father's—and the Empire's—actions. She said that the Moff had never even seen himself the "Hero of Ossus;" he had just been following the Sith's orders. According to her, following the conclusion of the massacre and Krayt's takeover of the Imperial government, Rulf was drunk for a week. Gunn noticed that he was never the same as he was before the attack, as he had become more of a recluse. The Moff later told to his daughter that while taking a killing shot was efficient means of dealing with someone, such an action could never be undone—a lesson he had learned on Ossus. By the time of Operation: Thunderstroke, Yage was becoming disillusioned with the Empire; he found the Sith under his command to be nothing more than monsters, venting his fears of becoming a Sith minion to Moffs Veed and Calixte, the former warning Yage of the treasonous nature of such thoughts. Yage thought of defecting to Fel's Empire-in-exile, but was prevented from doing so because he feared what would happen to his daughter if he did. Fed up with the Sith's lack of honor, Yage and his daughter at last decided to defect during the attack on Taivas, leading multiple disillusioned Sith-Imperials in returning their loyalty to Emperor Fel and later participating in Fel's assault on the Sith-Imperial capital of Coruscant. In addition to speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], Yage was fluent in [[Durese/Legends|Durese]], the [[Language/Legends|language]] of the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:IdentityCrisis-SWL19p12.jpg|thumb|right|280px|The error in ''Legacy 19'']] Moff Rulf Yage was first mentioned in [[2006]]'s [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]], the preview issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' series of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]]. Yage would go on to appear as a background character in [[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] and [[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]]. He would not be given any dialogue until the [[Star Wars: Legacy: Claws of the Dragon|''Claws of the Dragon'']] story arc; specifically, [[Legacy (2006) 14|parts one]], [[Legacy (2006) 15|two]], and [[Legacy (2006) 19|six]]. Additionally, he appeared in [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]], an expanded version of ''Legacy 0''. In all his appearances, save ''Legacy 8: Allies'', Yage was illustrated by artist [[Jan Duursema]]. In ''Allies'', [[Adam DeKraker]] took on the task of drawing Yage and the other Moffs. In ''Legacy 19: Claws of the Dragon, Part 6'', there are a few panels depicting Yage conversing with a holographic admiral. In the first of these, their text bubbles are switched—as a result, Yage is shown saying the admiral's line, and vice-versa. The error was not repeated in subsequent panels showing the rest of their conversation. This error was later corrected in the [[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 3: Claws of the Dragon|''Claws of the Dragon'' trade paperback]]. In [[Legacy (2006) 37|''Legacy'' (2006) 37]], [[Legacy — War 1|''Legacy—War'' 1]], [[Legacy — War 2|''Legacy—War'' 2]], and [[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy—War'' 4]], Yage is referred to as "Ranulf." When questioned about the issue, [[John Ostrander]] stated that "Ranulf" was a mistake, though "Rulf" could very well be short for "Ranulf."{{JCFCite|url=posts/31542244|board=Literature|thread=Legacy: War #3|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander01]]|date=2011-02-14|time=4:08 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200802122056/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-war-3.31364017/page-3}}
